codeReview.summary: Current HEAD still has the same blocking reconnect defect from the prior review. The persisted secure-store work is in place (`PersistedSecureStore()` remains wired through the mobile provider and cache repository), and the only follow-up repo change since `1a4c5fd` touching this feature area was the environment-library note update, but pairing, thread-detail, and thread-list reconnect flows still suppress follow-up timers while a reconnect attempt is marked in progress. That leaves longer bridge outages stuck after a single automatic retry, so trusted-session recovery and selected-thread/cached-thread catch-up still fall back to manual recovery.

issues.description: `_scheduleReconnect()` still returns early whenever `_isReconnectInProgress` is true, but `_restoreTrustedBridgeSession()` invokes `_scheduleReconnect()` on `connectivityUnavailable` at line 327 while `_runReconnect()` still holds `_isReconnectInProgress = true` until its `finally` block at lines 386-392. The result is still only one automatic reconnect attempt after startup/disconnect; if that retry also hits connectivity loss, no subsequent timer is armed and the trusted session requires manual recovery.

`_runReconnectCatchUp()` still tries to reschedule from its failure paths at lines 477-479 before `_isReconnectInProgress` is cleared in the `finally` block at lines 480-481, so `_scheduleReconnectCatchUp()` drops those follow-up attempts. After a live-stream disconnect, thread detail therefore gets at most one timed reconnect/catch-up attempt; longer outages still leave the selected thread stale until the user taps the retry affordance manually.

The thread-list reconnect path is still unchanged: `_runReconnect()` calls `_scheduleReconnect()` again on catch-up failure at lines 413-416 while `_isReconnectInProgress` remains true until lines 417-418. Because that reschedule is discarded, cached thread-list restoration and selected-thread relaunch recovery still stop after the first timed retry and can require a manual refresh during longer disconnects.

summary: Fail: the secure-store persistence portion remains fixed, but current HEAD still only auto-retries reconnect/catch-up once because follow-up timers are suppressed while reconnect work is in progress. I overwrote `.factory/validation/mobile-app/scrutiny/reviews/integration-mobile-persistence-and-auto-reconnect-fix.json` with this re-review report.
